The **Diocese of York** is an administrative division of the [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England"), part of the [Province of York](/wiki/Province_of_York "Province of York"). It covers the city of [York](/wiki/York "York"), the eastern part of [North Yorkshire](/wiki/North_Yorkshire "North Yorkshire"), and most of the [East Riding of Yorkshire](/wiki/East_Riding_of_Yorkshire "East Riding of Yorkshire").

The diocese is headed by the [archbishop of York](/wiki/Archbishop_of_York "Archbishop of York") and its [cathedral](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral") is [York Minster](/wiki/York_Minster "York Minster"). The diocese is divided into three [archdeaconries](/wiki/Archdeaconries "Archdeaconries") of [Cleveland](/wiki/Archdeaconry_of_Cleveland "Archdeaconry of Cleveland") in the north (with a [Bishop of Whitby](/wiki/Bishop_of_Whitby "Bishop of Whitby")), [the East Riding](/wiki/Archdeaconry_of_the_East_Riding "Archdeaconry of the East Riding") (with a [Bishop of Hull](/wiki/Bishop_of_Hull "Bishop of Hull")), and in the south-west the [Archdeaconry of York](/wiki/Archdeaconry_of_York "Archdeaconry of York") (with a [bishop of Selby](/wiki/Bishop_of_Selby "Bishop of Selby")).

The diocese was once much larger, covering [Yorkshire](/wiki/Yorkshire "Yorkshire"), [Nottinghamshire](/wiki/Nottinghamshire "Nottinghamshire") and [Derbyshire](/wiki/Derbyshire "Derbyshire") and parts of [Northumberland](/wiki/Northumberland "Northumberland"), [Lancashire](/wiki/Lancashire "Lancashire"), [Cumberland](/wiki/Cumberland "Cumberland") and [Westmorland](/wiki/Westmorland "Westmorland").

## Bishops

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bishops")]

The [diocesan](/wiki/Diocesan_bishop "Diocesan bishop") archbishop of York (currently [Stephen Cottrell](/wiki/Stephen_Cottrell "Stephen Cottrell")) is primarily supported by three [suffragan bishops](/wiki/Suffragan_bishop "Suffragan bishop"): the [bishop of Hull](/wiki/Bishop_of_Hull "Bishop of Hull") (founded 1891; currently [Eleanor Sanderson](/wiki/Eleanor_Sanderson "Eleanor Sanderson")[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)), the [bishop of Whitby](/wiki/Bishop_of_Whitby "Bishop of Whitby") (founded 1923; currently [Barry Hill](/wiki/Barry_Hill_(bishop) "Barry Hill (bishop)")[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)) and the bishop of Selby (founded 1939; currently [Flora Winfield](/wiki/Flora_Winfield "Flora Winfield")[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)). While not operating a formal [area scheme](/wiki/Area_scheme "Area scheme"), each suffragan takes informal responsibility for one archdeaconry (East Riding, Cleveland and York respectively). [Alternative episcopal oversight](/wiki/Alternative_episcopal_oversight "Alternative episcopal oversight") (for parishes in the diocese who reject the ministry of priests who are women) is provided by the [provincial episcopal visitor](/wiki/Provincial_episcopal_visitor "Provincial episcopal visitor") (PEV) the [bishop suffragan of Beverley](/wiki/Bishop_of_Beverley "Bishop of Beverley") (currently [Stephen Race](/wiki/Stephen_Race_(bishop) "Stephen Race (bishop)")); unlike in most dioceses, Beverley does not need to be licensed as an honorary assistant bishop since he is a suffragan in the diocese.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/Bishops_of_York_2022_%2852386173109%29.jpg/250px-Bishops_of_York_2022_%2852386173109%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bishops_of_York_2022_(52386173109).jpg)

Bishops of the Diocese of York in 2022, L to R: [Ferguson](/wiki/Paul_Ferguson_(bishop) "Paul Ferguson (bishop)"), Sanderson, Cottrell, and [Thomson](/wiki/John_Thomson_(bishop) "John Thomson (bishop)")

[David James](/wiki/David_James_(bishop) "David James (bishop)"), retired [bishop of Bradford](/wiki/Bishop_of_Bradford_(diocese) "Bishop of Bradford (diocese)")[[2]](#cite_note-2) and [Martin Wallace](/wiki/Martin_Wallace_(bishop) "Martin Wallace (bishop)"), retired [bishop suffragan of Selby](/wiki/Bishop_of_Selby "Bishop of Selby"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) live in [Beverley](/wiki/Beverley "Beverley") and [Bridlington](/wiki/Bridlington "Bridlington") respectively, but there is no evidence that either has been licensed as an honorary assistant bishop.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_York&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

In 1541, the archdeanery of [Richmond, North Yorkshire](/wiki/Richmond,_North_Yorkshire "Richmond, North Yorkshire"), which included part of the [Yorkshire Dales](/wiki/Yorkshire_Dales "Yorkshire Dales"), North Lancashire (including [Furness](/wiki/Furness "Furness")), the [southern part](/wiki/Barony_of_Kendal "Barony of Kendal") of [Westmorland](/wiki/Westmorland "Westmorland") and the [ward](/wiki/Ward_(politics) "Ward (politics)") of [Allerdale above Derwent](/wiki/Copeland,_Cumbria "Copeland, Cumbria") in [Cumberland](/wiki/Cumberland "Cumberland"), became part of the new [Diocese of Chester](/wiki/Diocese_of_Chester "Diocese of Chester"). (These areas later became parts of other dioceses.)

From the reign of [Henry I](/wiki/Henry_I_of_England "Henry I of England") to 1572 the [liberty](/wiki/Liberty "Liberty") or [county palatine](/wiki/County_palatine "County palatine") of [Hexhamshire](/wiki/Hexhamshire "Hexhamshire") was part of the diocese and also the archbishop was the lord palatine of the county. In 1572 the county palatine was abolished and transferred to Northumberland and the [Diocese of Durham](/wiki/Diocese_of_Durham "Diocese of Durham").

In 1836 the western part (corresponding broadly to the [West Riding](/wiki/West_Riding_of_Yorkshire "West Riding of Yorkshire")) was split into the [Ripon](/wiki/Diocese_of_Ripon_and_Leeds "Diocese of Ripon and Leeds") diocese, which was later subdivided into the dioceses of [Ripon and Leeds](/wiki/Diocese_of_Ripon_and_Leeds "Diocese of Ripon and Leeds"), [Bradford](/wiki/Diocese_of_Bradford "Diocese of Bradford"), and [Wakefield](/wiki/Diocese_of_Wakefield "Diocese of Wakefield") and now constitutes most of the [Diocese of Leeds](/wiki/Anglican_Diocese_of_Leeds "Anglican Diocese of Leeds"). In 1884 Nottinghamshire and [Derbyshire](/wiki/Derbyshire "Derbyshire") became part of the new [Diocese of Southwell](/wiki/Diocese_of_Southwell "Diocese of Southwell"), from which Derbyshire was split off again in 1927 to form the [Diocese of Derby](/wiki/Diocese_of_Derby "Diocese of Derby"). In 1914 the [Diocese of Sheffield](/wiki/Diocese_of_Sheffield "Diocese of Sheffield") (covering [South Yorkshire](/wiki/South_Yorkshire "South Yorkshire")) was split off as an independent diocese.

## Archdeacon for Generous Giving and Stewardship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_York&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Archdeacon for Generous Giving and Stewardship")]

[David Butterfield](/wiki/David_Butterfield "David Butterfield") resigned as [archdeacon of the East Riding](/wiki/Archdeacon_of_the_East_Riding "Archdeacon of the East Riding") on 26 May 2014 in order to be collated as "Archdeacon for Generous Giving and Stewardship" that 23 June,[[4]](#cite_note-4) a position he held until he retired on 1 July 2017.[[5]](#cite_note-ct8053-5)
